title: excoecaria laurocerasus var. elliptica
intro: Excoecaria laurocerasus var. elliptica, also known as the Tungoil or Poison Tree, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Australia and New Guinea. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can be utilized to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Excoecaria laurocerasus var. elliptica include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often extracted to create oils and tinctures for topical application.
